title: Dashboard Module A first batch: coverage heatmap + hit reason visualization
description: Establish Dashboard four-module layout structure and implement Module A first batch: coverage heatmap (which directories have rule coverage vs knowledge deserts) and hit reason visualization (why each rule was loaded: Glob/Description/Always-on). Depends on TASK-001 (RulesPayload.description_stubs and tier-aware matchReason). Dashboard is zero-write — no approve or mutate operations. Second-screen monitoring view.

files.change: New view component for Module A: Rule Topology. Renders two sections: CoverageHeatmap and HitReasonPanel. Reads rules data from SSE hook. No write operations — read-only display.

Visualize directory coverage: cross agents.meta.json scope_glob patterns against project file tree (available from existing server endpoint). Color directories by coverage density: green=covered, yellow=partial, gray=no rules. Use CSS grid or simple tree list with color-coded rows.

Display each rule with its activation tier badge: Always-on / Glob match / Description stub. For Glob rules: show matched pattern. For Description stubs: show description text. For Always-on: show 'Global'. Data sourced from RulesPayload including new description_stubs array from TASK-001.

Add route or tab for new rule-topology view. Establish four-module navigation skeleton (A: Rule Topology, B: Cognitive Forensic, C: Semantic Timeline, D: Historical Ledger) with placeholder stubs for B/C/D.
Export CoverageHeatmap and HitReasonPanel from component index

implementation: Read existing views (rules-tree.tsx, human-lock.tsx) to understand SSE hook usage and data access patterns
Read use-events.ts and api/client.ts to understand available data endpoints
Confirm RulesPayload type is accessible in dashboard (check shared package re-exports)
Create rule-topology.tsx view with two-panel layout: CoverageHeatmap (left/top) + HitReasonPanel (right/bottom)
Implement CoverageHeatmap: fetch agents meta nodes, extract scope_glob patterns, group by directory prefix, render colored directory tree
Implement HitReasonPanel: consume SSE rules events, display each rule entry with tier badge (Always-on/Glob/Description)
Add four-module navigation skeleton to app.tsx with A=active, B/C/D=placeholder stubs
Register rule-topology route in app router

reference.pattern: SSE hook consumption pattern from existing views
reference.examples: Follow rules-tree.tsx for SSE data consumption; follow human-lock.tsx for list rendering pattern

criteria: rule-topology view renders without runtime errors
CoverageHeatmap displays at least covered vs uncovered directory distinction
HitReasonPanel shows activation tier for each rule (Always-on / Glob / Description badges)
Description stubs display description text (not file content) from RulesPayload.description_stubs
Four-module navigation skeleton present in app.tsx (A active, B/C/D labeled stubs)
Zero write operations in new components (no POST/PUT/DELETE calls)
TypeScript compilation passes with zero new errors

rationale.chosen_approach: Module A first batch (S-complexity features) as proof of concept for four-module architecture before investing in M/L features
alternatives_considered: Build full Module A before starting B/C/D
Start with Module B (forensic) which depends on REC-2
decision_factors: Coverage heatmap + hit reason are S-complexity and directly enabled by TASK-001
Establishes navigation skeleton for all four modules
rationale.tradeoffs: B/C/D modules are stubs only — acceptable since they depend on future tasks (REC-2, semantic timeline data)

manual_checks: Open Dashboard rule-topology view and verify heatmap renders directory list
Verify tier badges display correctly for Always-on, Glob, and Description rules
Confirm no network write requests are made by the new components (check browser network tab)
success_metrics: CoverageHeatmap renders directory tree with coverage colors in under 200ms after data load
HitReasonPanel correctly distinguishes all 3 tier types with distinct visual badges

risks.description: RulesPayload.description_stubs field not yet available if TASK-001 not completed first
risks.mitigation: Defensive: handle undefined description_stubs with empty array fallback; show placeholder when stubs absent
risks.fallback: Render tier badge as 'Unknown' when stubs array missing

risks.description: agents.meta.json scope_glob parsing for coverage heatmap requires directory-level grouping logic
risks.mitigation: Use minimatch negation patterns to infer directory prefix from glob like 'src/auth/**/*.ts' → 'src/auth'
risks.fallback: Display full glob string instead of directory group

interfaces.name: DirectoryCoverage
interfaces.definition: { path: string; covered: boolean; matchingGlobs: string[]; density: 'full' | 'partial' | 'none' }
interfaces.purpose: Coverage status for a single directory path

functions.purpose: Cross scope_glob patterns against project file tree to compute per-directory coverage
functions.returns: Array of DirectoryCoverage objects for rendering

classes.name: CoverageHeatmap
classes.purpose: React component rendering directory coverage grid with color coding

classes.name: HitReasonPanel
classes.purpose: React component rendering per-rule tier badges from RulesPayload
methods: render tier badge by activation type
